name: First Priority
description_short: First Priority Federal Credit Union is a member-owned, not-for-profit credit union serving West Virginia since 1925, offering auto loans, savings accounts, certificates, and digital banking services.
description_long:
First Priority Federal Credit Union has operated as a member-owned financial cooperative since 1925, establishing itself as a local institution focused on serving its community in West Virginia. The credit union maintains a physical presence in Huntington with limited operations in Barboursville, complemented by robust digital infrastructure including a mobile app, remote deposit capabilities, and 24/7 online services. Their service model emphasizes personalized member relationships and understanding local financial needs, positioning themselves as a community-focused alternative to traditional banks.

The credit union offers a comprehensive range of financial products including auto loans with rates advertised as low as 2.99%, personal lines of credit, savings accounts (Share Builders with tiered rates up to 0.075% APY), money market accounts, share certificates (ranging from 6-month to 36-month terms with rates up to 4.00% APY), and member-exclusive credit cards and insurance services. They provide modern banking conveniences such as bill pay with AutoPay and eBill options, ACH electronic funds transfers, remote check deposit via mobile app, debit cards, and ATM access. Their rate structure emphasizes member benefits across all services.

What distinguishes First Priority FCU is their explicit emphasis on member-first philosophy, with a 99-year operational history and local community focus. The credit union advertises competitive rates across all product categories and offers member-exclusive pricing on insurance and credit card services. They maintain transparent rate disclosure on their website and provide straightforward access to services through multiple channels—in-person during business hours (8:30 AM–5:00 PM Monday–Friday), phone, email, and comprehensive digital platforms.

The primary limitation for prospective members is geographic restriction to the West Virginia area with limited physical branch presence (currently consolidating Barboursville operations). Rates, while competitive, require direct contact for current terms and exact APYs on specific loan products. The credit union's smaller scale compared to national banks means fewer ATM locations outside the local market and potentially limited product customization compared to larger financial institutions.
services: Auto loans and refinancing with competitive rates
Share savings accounts with tiered dividend rates
Money market accounts with tiered rates ($1,000–$25,000+)
Mobile app with remote check deposit
Bill pay with AutoPay and eBill options
pros: Auto loan rates advertised as low as 2.99% APR
Share certificates offering up to 4.00% APY for 6-month terms ($1,000 minimum)
Member-exclusive insurance services and credit card benefits
Remote mobile check deposit and 24/7 digital banking access
99-year operating history (since 1925) with established community presence
Bill pay with AutoPay scheduling and eBill options with 84-month payment history tracking
Multiple savings account tiers with tiered dividend rates up to 0.15% APY
cons: Limited physical branch locations (only Huntington office operational; Barboursville closing May 1st with limited hours)
Geographic restriction—primarily serves West Virginia area, limiting accessibility for non-local members
Debit cards explicitly cannot be used for hotels or car rentals, requiring separate credit card application
Rates and specific loan terms require direct contact to verify current offerings
Smaller institution means fewer ATM locations and less extensive service network compared to national credit unions
best_for: West Virginia residents seeking competitive auto loan rates and local relationship banking
Members prioritizing member-owned cooperative structure with community-focused service
Savers looking for certificate rates competitive with national rates on smaller balances
Customers preferring digital-first banking with mobile check deposit and bill pay automation
diagnosis: First Priority FCU is best for West Virginia-based consumers seeking a member-owned credit union with competitive auto loan rates, certificate yields, and personalized service, but is limited primarily to local geographic markets. The institution's main caveat is its restricted branch footprint and requirement to contact directly for current rate verification, making it less suitable for those seeking national accessibility or digitally-only relationship management.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
meta_title: First Priority — Credit Union in Huntington, WV | CreditDoc
meta_description: First Priority — NCUA-insured credit union in Huntington, WV. Member-owned, not-for-profit. Loans, savings, and member services.
